content.text: Audit Evidence for GenAI Controls and Logs

An audit of a GenAI system is fundamentally an evidence problem: regulators, internal auditors, and external certification bodies cannot inspect a probabilistic model directly. They examine the artefacts that prove your controls were designed correctly and operated as intended during the audit period. This sub-subtopic covers what evidence to collect, how to structure it for auditability, and how to assemble it into an audit package.
Auditor mindset
: auditors do not take your word that a control existed. They follow a chain: policy → procedure → implementation evidence → log sample → outcome. A gap in any link breaks the chain.
Evidence Architecture

attrs.alt: Audit Evidence Architecture for GenAI Systems diagram

content.text: Evidence Categories
Six categories of evidence collectively cover the control surface of a production GenAI system:
Category
What it proves
Primary source
Retention
Inference gateway logs
Every model call was logged; guardrails fired when triggered
API gateway middleware
3 years
Control test results
Each control was tested and the outcome preserved
Control test runner (CI/CD)
3 years
Model registry events
Only approved model versions were deployed; change control followed
MLflow / SageMaker / internal registry
Duration of model in prod + 3 years
Human-in-the-loop records
High-risk outputs were reviewed by a qualified human before delivery
HITL workflow service
7 years
Access control audit log
Only authorised principals accessed model endpoints and training data
IAM / SIEM
3 years
Bias / fairness eval reports
Periodic fairness evaluations were conducted with acceptable results
Evaluation pipeline
3 years
Python Implementation

import hashlib, json
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime, date
from enum import Enum
from typing import Optional, Any

class EvidenceCategory(str, Enum):
    INFERENCE_LOG     = "inference_log"
    CONTROL_TEST      = "control_test"
    MODEL_REGISTRY    = "model_registry"
    HITL_RECORD       = "hitl_record"
    ACCESS_LOG        = "access_log"
    BIAS_EVAL         = "bias_eval"
    CONFIG_SNAPSHOT   = "config_snapshot"
    CERT_DOCUMENT     = "cert_document"

class RetentionClass(str, Enum):
    STANDARD  = "3y"    # default operational logs
    EXTENDED  = "7y"    # regulated data (HIPAA, financial)
    PERMANENT = "perm"  # legal holds; some safety incidents

@dataclass
class AuditEvidenceRecord:
    record_id: str
    category: EvidenceCategory
    control_id: Optional[str]       # maps to a control in the library
    source_system: str              # e.g. "inference-gateway-prod"
    collected_at: datetime
    retention: RetentionClass
    payload: bytes | dict           # raw bytes or structured object
    sha256: str = field(init=False)
    metadata: dict = field(default_factory=dict)

    def __post_init__(self):
        raw = (json.dumps(self.payload, sort_keys=True).encode()
               if isinstance(self.payload, dict)
               else self.payload)
        self.sha256 = hashlib.sha256(raw).hexdigest()

    def index_entry(self) -> dict:
        """Lightweight record for the Postgres index (no payload)."""
        return {
            "record_id":     self.record_id,
            "category":      self.category.value,
            "control_id":    self.control_id,
            "source_system": self.source_system,
            "collected_at":  self.collected_at.isoformat(),
            "retention":     self.retention.value,
            "sha256":        self.sha256,
            **self.metadata,
        }
Inference Gateway Log Records
Every inference request generates a log record. Store metadata and hashes —
not
the raw prompt and response text — to satisfy auditability without creating a data minimisation violation under GDPR Article 5(1)(c):

import uuid
from datetime import datetime

def create_inference_log_record(
    prompt_id: str,
    user_id: str,
    model_id: str,
    model_version: str,
    input_tokens: int,
    output_tokens: int,
    latency_ms: float,
    guardrail_triggered: bool,
    guardrail_action: Optional[str],   # "blocked", "redacted", None
    response_sha256: str,              # hash of the response text (not the text itself)
) -> AuditEvidenceRecord:
    """
    Creates an inference log record suitable for audit evidence.

    The response text is NOT stored — only its hash and metadata.
    This balances auditability with data minimisation (GDPR Art.5(1)(c)).
    """
    return AuditEvidenceRecord(
        record_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        category=EvidenceCategory.INFERENCE_LOG,
        control_id=None,   # inference logs cross-cut multiple controls
        source_system="inference-gateway-prod",
        collected_at=datetime.utcnow(),
        retention=RetentionClass.STANDARD,
        payload={
            "prompt_id":           prompt_id,
            "user_id":             user_id,
            "model_id":            model_id,
            "model_version":       model_version,
            "input_tokens":        input_tokens,
            "output_tokens":       output_tokens,
            "latency_ms":          latency_ms,
            "guardrail_triggered": guardrail_triggered,
            "guardrail_action":    guardrail_action,
            "response_sha256":     response_sha256,
            # Deliberate omissions: prompt text, response text (data minimisation)
        },
        metadata={"schema_version": "inference-log-v2"},
    )
Human-in-the-Loop Decision Records
HITL records are the most scrutinised category in a regulatory audit of high-risk AI systems (EU AI Act Annex III). The rationale field is not optional — auditors will read it:

@dataclass
class HITLDecisionRecord:
    """Human-in-the-loop review decision for high-stakes AI outputs."""
    review_id: str
    request_id: str           # the inference request that triggered review
    reviewer_id: str          # IAM principal of the human reviewer
    decision: str             # "approved" | "rejected" | "modified"
    rationale: str            # free text — required for audit trail
    reviewed_at: datetime
    output_sha256: str        # hash of the AI output that was reviewed
    modified_output_sha256: Optional[str] = None  # set if decision == "modified"

    def to_evidence_record(self) -> AuditEvidenceRecord:
        return AuditEvidenceRecord(
            record_id=f"hitl-{self.review_id}",
            category=EvidenceCategory.HITL_RECORD,
            control_id="CTR-HITL-001",
            source_system="hitl-workflow-service",
            collected_at=self.reviewed_at,
            retention=RetentionClass.EXTENDED,   # reviewer decisions are regulatory evidence
            payload={
                "review_id":              self.review_id,
                "request_id":             self.request_id,
                "reviewer_id":            self.reviewer_id,
                "decision":               self.decision,
                "rationale":              self.rationale,
                "output_sha256":          self.output_sha256,
                "modified_output_sha256": self.modified_output_sha256,
            },
        )
Audit Package Builder
When an audit is announced, the package builder queries the evidence store, filters by audit scope, and produces a signed ZIP archive with a manifest:

import zipfile, io, json
from datetime import date

class AuditPackageBuilder:
    """
    Assembles evidence records into a signed, indexed audit package
    for a given audit scope (date range + control set).
    """
    def __init__(self, evidence_store):
        self._store = evidence_store   # any object with .query(filters) → list[AuditEvidenceRecord]

    def build(
        self,
        audit_name: str,
        period_start: date,
        period_end: date,
        control_ids: list[str] | None = None,
        categories: list[EvidenceCategory] | None = None,
    ) -> bytes:
        """Returns a ZIP archive (bytes) containing all evidence + manifest."""
        records = self._store.query(
            period_start=period_start,
            period_end=period_end,
            control_ids=control_ids,
            categories=categories,
        )

        manifest = {
            "audit_name":    audit_name,
            "period":        f"{period_start} to {period_end}",
            "generated_at":  datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            "record_count":  len(records),
            "controls_covered": list({r.control_id for r in records if r.control_id}),
            "categories":    list({r.category.value for r in records}),
            "records":       [r.index_entry() for r in records],
        }

        buf = io.BytesIO()
        with zipfile.ZipFile(buf, "w", zipfile.ZIP_DEFLATED) as zf:
            zf.writestr("manifest.json", json.dumps(manifest, indent=2))
            for rec in records:
                raw = (json.dumps(rec.payload).encode()
                       if isinstance(rec.payload, dict)
                       else rec.payload)
                zf.writestr(f"evidence/{rec.record_id}.json", raw)

        return buf.getvalue()

    def coverage_report(
        self,
        period_start: date,
        period_end: date,
        required_controls: list[str],
    ) -> dict:
        """
        Returns a dict mapping each required control to whether it has
        at least one evidence record in the period.
        Surfaced in audit readiness reviews to find gaps before the auditor does.
        """
        records = self._store.query(period_start=period_start, period_end=period_end)
        evidenced = {r.control_id for r in records if r.control_id}
        return {
            ctrl: {
                "covered": ctrl in evidenced,
                "record_count": sum(1 for r in records if r.control_id == ctrl),
            }
            for ctrl in required_controls
        }
Evidence Quality Requirements
An evidence record is only useful in an audit if it meets four quality criteria:
Criterion
What it means
How to achieve it
Immutability
The record cannot be modified after creation
S3 Object Lock (WORM); append-only Postgres table; no UPDATE/DELETE on audit rows
Integrity
Tampering is detectable
SHA-256 hash stored separately from payload; re-verify hash on export
Completeness
No gaps in the audit period
Coverage report before the audit; alert if daily record count drops to zero
Authenticity
The record genuinely came from the stated system
Signed at source (HMAC with system key); chain-of-custody log for evidence store access
Sampling Strategy for Large Log Volumes
Inference gateways can generate millions of records per day. Auditors typically sample rather than inspect every record. Design your evidence collection to support stratified sampling:
Stratify by guardrail outcome

content: : always preserve 100% of records where a guardrail fired. These are the most auditor-relevant records.

content.text: Daily count checksum

content: : store a daily aggregate record count with a SHA-256 of all record IDs for that day. Auditors can verify population completeness without inspecting every row.

content.text: Random sample archive

content: : each day, tag a random 0.1% of records as the 'auditor sample'. Pre-index these for fast retrieval during audit fieldwork.

content.text: High-risk user population

content: : for regulated use cases (financial advice, medical information), preserve all records, not just samples.

content.text:
import random
from datetime import date, timedelta

def tag_daily_audit_sample(
    records: list[AuditEvidenceRecord],
    sample_rate: float = 0.001,
    always_include_categories: set[EvidenceCategory] | None = None,
) -> list[AuditEvidenceRecord]:
    """
    Tag records as part of the daily auditor sample.
    Records in always_include_categories are always tagged regardless of rate.
    """
    always_include_categories = always_include_categories or {
        EvidenceCategory.HITL_RECORD,
        EvidenceCategory.CONTROL_TEST,
    }
    sampled = []
    for rec in records:
        if rec.category in always_include_categories:
            rec.metadata["audit_sample"] = True
            sampled.append(rec)
        elif random.random() < sample_rate:
            rec.metadata["audit_sample"] = True
            sampled.append(rec)
    return sampled
Log Schema Design for Auditability
Every inference log record should carry these fields — auditors will ask for all of them:
Pre-Audit Readiness Check
Run a coverage report at least 30 days before the audit period ends to surface evidence gaps while there is still time to remediate:

def pre_audit_readiness_check(
    builder: AuditPackageBuilder,
    period_start: date,
    period_end: date,
    required_controls: list[str],
) -> None:
    coverage = builder.coverage_report(period_start, period_end, required_controls)
    gaps = [ctrl for ctrl, info in coverage.items() if not info["covered"]]

    if gaps:
        print(f"EVIDENCE GAPS ({len(gaps)} controls missing evidence):")
        for ctrl in gaps:
            print(f"  {ctrl}: 0 records in period {period_start} → {period_end}")
        print("\nAction required: run retrospective evidence collection or document exception.")
    else:
        total_records = sum(info["record_count"] for info in coverage.values())
        print(f"All {len(required_controls)} controls have evidence. "
              f"Total records: {total_records}.")
Common Evidence Gaps Found in GenAI Audits
Gap
Root cause
Remediation
No log for a specific date window
Log rotation deleted records before retention period elapsed
Set S3 lifecycle policy to enforce minimum retention; alert if daily count = 0
Control test results exist but no evidence payload
Tests stored pass/fail only, not the raw test output
Attach the HTTP response or assertion payload to each test result record
HITL records missing rationale
UI allowed reviewers to approve without filling rationale
Make rationale a required field; block submission if empty
Model registry shows no approval records for a deployment
Deployment was pushed directly to prod without approval workflow
Enforce registry gate in CI/CD; make direct push to prod impossible
Fairness evaluation reports older than 12 months
Evaluation not re-run after model update
Trigger evaluation pipeline automatically on every model version change
Summary

Audit readiness for GenAI controls is built on six evidence categories: inference logs, control test results, model registry events, HITL decision records, access logs, and fairness evaluation reports. Each record must be immutable, integrity-protected, complete, and authentic. Design inference logs to capture control outcomes and response hashes — not raw text — to balance auditability with data minimisation. Run a coverage report 30 days before audit fieldwork begins so that gaps can be remediated before the auditor arrives, not discovered during it.
